_comment: User-editable anti-pattern rules for pre-tool-course-correct.py. Set enabled=false to disable a rule.

rules.description: Use the Grep tool instead of grep/rg in Bash
rules.message: Use the Grep tool instead of `grep`/`rg` in Bash — better UX, token-efficient, respects .gitignore. Exception: pipeline filtering (`cmd | grep pattern`) and advanced modes (-P PCRE, -A/-B/-C context) are fine in Bash.

rules.description: Use the Read tool instead of cat for reading files
rules.message: Use the Read tool instead of `cat` to read files — it provides line numbers and is token-efficient. `cat` is fine in heredocs (`cat << EOF`) and pipelines (`cmd | cat`).

rules.description: Use the Read tool with offset/limit instead of head/tail
rules.message: Use the Read tool with `offset` and `limit` parameters instead of `head`/`tail`. Exceptions: pipeline use (`cmd | head`) and `tail -f` (live follow) are fine.

rules.description: Use the Glob tool instead of find for file discovery
rules.message: Use the Glob tool instead of `find` for file discovery — faster and respects .gitignore. Use Bash `find` only for operations Glob can't do: `-exec`, `-delete`, `-newer`, `-mtime`, `-size`, `-perm`.

Use `bun` instead of `npm` and `bunx` instead of `npx` — significantly faster. Examples: `bun install`, `bun run build`, `bunx tsc`. Exception: `npm publish`, `npm pack`, `npm version` have no bun equivalent.

rules.description: Use uv instead of pip/pip3 for package management
rules.message: Use `uv` instead of `pip`/`pip3` — dramatically faster with automatic venv management. Examples: `uv add <pkg>` (project), `uv pip install <pkg>` (bare), `uv run python script.py`.

rules.description: Use mise instead of nvm for Node version management
rules.message: Use `mise` instead of `nvm` for Node version management. Examples: `mise install node@20`, `mise use node@lts`, `mise use --global node@20`.

rules.description: Use mise instead of pyenv for Python version management
rules.message: Use `mise` instead of `pyenv` for Python version management. Examples: `mise install python@3.12`, `mise use python@3.12`.

rules.description: Use mise instead of rustup for Rust version pinning
rules.message: Use `mise` instead of `rustup` for Rust version pinning. Exception: `rustup component add`, `rustup target add`, `rustup update`, and `rustup show` are fine as-is.

failure_learning.message_template:
[course-correct] This exact command has failed {count} times in the last {window} minutes. Consider a different approach — check the path/syntax, use a dedicated tool, or break the command into smaller steps.

Failing command: {preview}
